summary: `drift.rs` re-spells the lock filename at five sites while `LOCK_FILENAME` now holds its one home — finish the consolidation the workspace-dir pass left short

edit.description: Read `crate::LOCK_FILENAME` at the five `workspace_dir.join("lock.toml")` sites — `read_prior_provenance` (1436), `config_stale` (1812), `source_deps` (1939), `emit_owned_targets` (2075), `read_declarations` (2738) — the same use-site read `import.rs` already takes. The module's `//!` header and the `./.temper`/`parent()` comment at 716 name the concept in prose, not as literals; leave them.

acceptance: One home spells the lock filename: `rg 'lock\.toml' src/` returns, outside comments, `src/lib.rs`'s const and `builtin_lock.rs`'s distinct `src/builtin_lock.toml` embed alone. Behavior unchanged — `cargo test` green with no test edited, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
notes: Sweep finding, 169e62f..27a2e28. 23c31c4 homed `.temper` and `lock.toml` in lib.rs, converting the four modules that held *consts*; drift.rs holds none, so both that scope and the entry's const-shaped acceptance missed its five inline `join("lock.toml")` calls. Pure refactor — no behavior moves, so no new test; the existing suites are the regression net. Test fixtures spell the literal inline: out of scope, test data rather than a const surface. scoped at 27a2e28.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested at 27a2e28: no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, release.yml still defers darwin. scoped at b676656.
